title: Module 2 Project: Department Participation Scorecard
summary: Build an all-department scorecard with course, enrollment, and distinct-student counts in six cumulative steps.

sketchBlocks.title: Project Overview: Department Participation Scorecard
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown: In this project, you will create a comprehensive department participation scorecard for a school. The goal is to ensure that every department is represented, even if it has no courses or enrollments. You'll use SQL to join tables and calculate accurate counts for courses, enrollments, and unique students per department. This project will help you understand how to preserve data integrity and report accuracy using LEFT JOINs and aggregate functions.

quizDraft.title: Preserve Every Department
quizDraft.prompt: Write a query to list all departments, ensuring that even those without courses are included. Use a LEFT JOIN to connect departments with courses.
quizDraft.hint: Use LEFT JOIN to ensure all departments are listed, even those without courses.

help.concept: LEFT JOIN allows you to include all rows from the left table, even if there are no matching rows in the right table.
help.hint_1: Start with the departments table and LEFT JOIN with courses.
help.hint_2: Ensure that the Arts department appears in the results, even if it has no courses.

quizDraft.title: Count Courses Correctly
quizDraft.prompt: Modify your query to count the number of courses per department, ensuring that departments with no courses show a count of zero.
quizDraft.hint: Use COUNT(DISTINCT c.id) to count courses per department.

help.concept: COUNT(DISTINCT ...) counts unique occurrences, which helps avoid inflating counts due to joins.
help.hint_1: Group by department and use COUNT(DISTINCT c.id) for course count.
help.hint_2: Ensure departments with no courses show a count of zero.

quizDraft.starterCode:
SELECT d.name, COUNT(DISTINCT c.id) AS course_count
FROM departments d
LEFT JOIN courses c ON d.id = c.department_id
GROUP BY d.name;
quizDraft.solutionCode:
SELECT d.name, COUNT(DISTINCT c.id) AS course_count
FROM departments d
LEFT JOIN courses c ON d.id = c.department_id
GROUP BY d.name;

quizDraft.title: Extend to Enrollment Records
quizDraft.prompt: Extend your query to include enrollments, ensuring all departments are preserved. Use a LEFT JOIN to connect courses with enrollments.
quizDraft.hint: Use LEFT JOIN to connect courses with enrollments while preserving all departments.

help.concept: LEFT JOIN ensures that all rows from the left table are included, even if there are no matches in the right table.
help.hint_1: Start with the existing query and add a LEFT JOIN to enrollments.
help.hint_2: Ensure that departments with no enrollments still appear in the results.

quizDraft.starterCode:
SELECT d.name, COUNT(DISTINCT c.id) AS course_count
FROM departments d
LEFT JOIN courses c ON d.id = c.department_id
LEFT JOIN enrollments e ON c.id = e.course_id
GROUP BY d.name;
quizDraft.solutionCode:
SELECT d.name, COUNT(DISTINCT c.id) AS course_count
FROM departments d
LEFT JOIN courses c ON d.id = c.department_id
LEFT JOIN enrollments e ON c.id = e.course_id
GROUP BY d.name;

quizDraft.title: Count Enrollment Rows
quizDraft.prompt: Add a count of enrollment rows per department to your query, ensuring the department grain is maintained.
quizDraft.hint: Use COUNT(e.id) to count enrollment rows per department.

help.concept: COUNT(column) counts non-null occurrences of the column, useful for counting related records.
help.hint_1: Add COUNT(e.id) to count enrollments per department.
help.hint_2: Ensure the department grain is maintained in the results.

quizDraft.starterCode:
SELECT d.name, COUNT(DISTINCT c.id) AS course_count, COUNT(e.id) AS enrollment_count
FROM departments d
LEFT JOIN courses c ON d.id = c.department_id
LEFT JOIN enrollments e ON c.id = e.course_id
GROUP BY d.name;
quizDraft.solutionCode:
SELECT d.name, COUNT(DISTINCT c.id) AS course_count, COUNT(e.id) AS enrollment_count
FROM departments d
LEFT JOIN courses c ON d.id = c.department_id
LEFT JOIN enrollments e ON c.id = e.course_id
GROUP BY d.name;

quizDraft.title: Count Unique Students
quizDraft.prompt: Modify your query to count unique students per department, ensuring each student is counted only once per department.
quizDraft.hint: Use COUNT(DISTINCT e.student_id) to count unique students per department.

help.concept: COUNT(DISTINCT ...) ensures each unique value is counted once, useful for counting unique entities.
help.hint_1: Add COUNT(DISTINCT e.student_id) for unique student count.
help.hint_2: Ensure each student is counted only once per department.

quizDraft.starterCode:
SELECT d.name, COUNT(DISTINCT c.id) AS course_count, COUNT(e.id) AS enrollment_count, COUNT(DISTINCT e.student_id) AS student_count
FROM departments d
LEFT JOIN courses c ON d.id = c.department_id
LEFT JOIN enrollments e ON c.id = e.course_id
GROUP BY d.name;
quizDraft.solutionCode:
SELECT d.name, COUNT(DISTINCT c.id) AS course_count, COUNT(e.id) AS enrollment_count, COUNT(DISTINCT e.student_id) AS student_count
FROM departments d
LEFT JOIN courses c ON d.id = c.department_id
LEFT JOIN enrollments e ON c.id = e.course_id
GROUP BY d.name;

quizDraft.title: Rank the Scorecard Predictably
quizDraft.prompt: Sort the final scorecard by enrollment count descending, then by department name ascending.
quizDraft.hint: Use ORDER BY to sort by enrollment_count descending and department name ascending.

help.concept: ORDER BY allows you to sort results based on specified columns and order.
help.hint_1: Use ORDER BY enrollment_count DESC, d.name ASC.
help.hint_2: Ensure the scorecard is sorted predictably by enrollment count and department name.

quizDraft.starterCode:
SELECT d.name, COUNT(DISTINCT c.id) AS course_count, COUNT(e.id) AS enrollment_count, COUNT(DISTINCT e.student_id) AS student_count
FROM departments d
LEFT JOIN courses c ON d.id = c.department_id
LEFT JOIN enrollments e ON c.id = e.course_id
GROUP BY d.name
ORDER BY enrollment_count DESC, d.name ASC;
quizDraft.solutionCode:
SELECT d.name, COUNT(DISTINCT c.id) AS course_count, COUNT(e.id) AS enrollment_count, COUNT(DISTINCT e.student_id) AS student_count
FROM departments d
LEFT JOIN courses c ON d.id = c.department_id
LEFT JOIN enrollments e ON c.id = e.course_id
GROUP BY d.name
ORDER BY enrollment_count DESC, d.name ASC;
